comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *renovate[bot]*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> @cevich I think it would make sense to add some special case magic for golangci-lint update PRs to not run tests, golangci-lint is only used in the validate task so maybe just automatically adding [CI:DOCS] in the title is good enough?

@Luap99 that's a fantastic idea.  Would you like to give it a try?

> > @cevich I think it would make sense to add some special case magic for golangci-lint update PRs to not run tests, golangci-lint is only used in the validate task so maybe just automatically adding [CI:DOCS] in the title is good enough?
> 
> @Luap99 that's a fantastic idea. Would you like to give it a try?

I can but I guess this something that only applies to the podman repo so must this be set in the config here or in the global config in the automation repo?

It's a good question.  Actually both podman and buildah use it, and maybe skopeo and image as well.  I know podman gets the version from it's `Makefile` but the other repos. look it up from `go.mod`.  A global package rule should be able to match both.

If you're not interested, let me know.  I'm just motivated to have others participate in massaging Renovate.  Thus-far most people refuse to touch it with a 10-foot pole :smile:
I gave it a try in https://github.com/containers/automation/pull/153 but without any way of testing these changes it is hard to know what options to use.
Thanks I'll take a look.  Yeah, there's no good way to test it besides validation.  The good news is, the barrier to merging fixes in that repo. is very lo :smile:
